title: AI Developer Radar｜Daily｜2026-06-09
summary: Developer attention today is split between two strong currents: AI agent frameworks and local LLM inference tooling. The sustained hot streak of 'pm-skills' (13k+ stars, +798 in 24h) and the acceleration of 'whichllm' (+630 in 24h) suggest builders are actively seeking structured agentic capabilities and hardware-aware local model selection. Meanwhile, 'AiToEarn' (+873 in 24h) broke out suddenly, indicating a new wave of interest in AI-powered content monetization platforms. Computer vision tools like 'supervision' and 'opencv' remain steady but not accelerating. On Hacker News, a thread titled 'LLMs are eroding my software engineering career' garnered 1119 votes, reflecting developer anxiety about AI's impact on jobs—a sentiment that may be fueling interest in agent frameworks that augment rather than replace. The absence of major launches on Product Hunt today suggests the attention is concentrated on GitHub projects rather than new products.
dataNotes: GitHub API 只提供当前 stars，总量变化来自本项目保存的历史 snapshot。
daily delta 使用约 24h 前 snapshot，weekly delta 使用约 7 天前 snapshot。
Accelerating 只展示至少 3 天历史基线且今日 delta 超过前 3 日均值 2 倍的项目。
Potential Score 为规则评分，用于排序，不代表项目质量结论。

repository.name: whichllm
repository.description: Find the local LLM that actually runs and performs best on your hardware. Ranked by real, recency-aware benchmarks, not parameter count. One command, run it instantly.

hotProjects.whyItMatters: Local LLM / Inference 方向保持持续热门，24h stars +630，总 stars 3,800，适合看近期 release、commit 或社区讨论是否解释了增长。
hotProjects.developerInsight: 重点看部署复杂度、性能边界和模型兼容性，判断是否能进入本地 AI 应用栈。

llmSummary.oneLiner: A CLI tool that auto-detects your hardware and ranks local LLMs by real, recency-aware benchmarks — not just parameter count.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Choosing the best local LLM for your specific hardware is tedious and error-prone. whichllm automates hardware detection, VRAM estimation, and benchmark-aware ranking so you get the best model that actually runs on your machine with one command.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 3,800 stars and a 24-hour delta of +630, whichllm is experiencing a sustained surge in attention. The project has a recent release (within 30 days) and active commits within the last 7 days, indicating ongoing development and community engagement. The acceleration ratio is low (1.0), suggesting steady organic growth rather than a sudden spike.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release and continuous commits likely maintain momentum, and the tool addresses a clear pain point for the growing local LLM community.
llmSummary.whyTrending: whichllm solves a real, common problem for anyone running LLMs locally: figuring out which model works best on their hardware. Its one-command simplicity, live benchmark integration, and GPU simulation for purchase planning make it immediately useful. The combination of practical utility and polished documentation drives word-of-mouth and social sharing.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you run local LLMs, install whichllm to instantly find the best model for your hardware. It saves hours of trial and error and provides actionable insights for hardware upgrades.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward tooling that abstracts away the complexity of local LLM deployment. Developers should expect more 'auto-pilot' tools that handle hardware detection, model selection, and optimization, reducing friction for end users and enabling broader adoption of local AI.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI developers, ML engineers, hobbyists running local LLMs, and anyone evaluating hardware for local inference (e.g., planning a GPU purchase).
llmSummary.riskNotes: The tool relies on HuggingFace API and external benchmark sources; if those change or become unavailable, functionality may degrade. The scoring system uses heuristics and confidence levels that may not always reflect real-world performance. Users should verify critical decisions with their own benchmarks.

repository.name: pm-skills
repository.description: PM Skills Marketplace: 100+ agentic skills, commands, and plugins — from discovery to strategy, execution, launch, and growth.

hotProjects.whyItMatters: AI Agent Framework 方向保持持续热门，24h stars +798，总 stars 13,158，适合看近期 release、commit 或社区讨论是否解释了增长。
hotProjects.developerInsight: 可以用一个最小样例验证 API、文档和集成成本，再决定是否纳入长期技术观察。

llmSummary.oneLiner: PM Skills Marketplace is an open-source collection of 100+ agentic skills, commands, and plugins for AI assistants, turning generic AI into a structured product management co-pilot.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Generic AI assistants lack domain-specific frameworks for product management. This repo provides ready-to-use PM skills (e.g., discovery, strategy, execution) that encode proven methodologies, enabling AI to guide users through structured workflows instead of just generating text.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has 13,158 stars and gained 798 stars in the last 24 hours, indicating strong and sustained attention. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), meaning growth is steady rather than explosive, but the consistent high velocity suggests deep community interest. This is not a flash-in-the-pan; it's a project with staying power.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release (within 30 days) and active commits (within 7 days) show ongoing maintenance, but the star velocity is likely driven by word-of-mouth and the growing need for structured AI workflows in product management.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It addresses a clear pain point: product managers want AI that understands their domain, not just a chatbot. The marketplace model (skills, commands, plugins) is easy to adopt, and the integration with Claude Code and Cowork lowers the barrier. The MIT license and PR-friendly culture encourage community contributions.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a blueprint for building domain-specific AI skill marketplaces. Developers can learn how to structure skills, commands, and plugins for other verticals (e.g., marketing, engineering, design) and see how to make them compatible with multiple AI assistants.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The project signals a shift from general-purpose AI to specialized, framework-driven agents. For AI developers, this means investing in domain-specific skill repositories could be a high-impact strategy. The plugin architecture is portable across assistants, reducing lock-in risk.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Product managers, AI assistant power users, developers building AI workflows, and anyone using Claude Code, Cowork, Codex CLI, or other AI tools for product development.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Heavy reliance on Claude-specific commands may limit adoption on other platforms. The quality of skills depends on community contributions, which could vary. Also, the project is young and may not have long-term maintenance guarantees.

repository.name: supervision
repository.description: We write your reusable computer vision tools. 💜

hotProjects.whyItMatters: MCP / Tool Calling 方向保持持续热门，24h stars +728，总 stars 42,680，适合看近期 release、commit 或社区讨论是否解释了增长。
hotProjects.developerInsight: 重点看协议适配、工具注册和真实示例，判断它是否能降低 agent 接工具的成本。

llmSummary.oneLiner: A reusable, model-agnostic Python toolkit for computer vision tasks like annotation, tracking, and dataset management.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Eliminates boilerplate code for common CV workflows (detection, segmentation, tracking, dataset conversion) so developers can focus on model application rather than infrastructure.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 42,680 stars and a 24-hour delta of +728, this project shows sustained high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), indicating steady organic interest rather than a sudden spike. It's actively maintained (commits within 7 days) and has a large community (3,811 forks, 104 open issues). The consistent daily star gain suggests it's a go-to resource for CV practitioners.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent commits and ongoing community engagement (Discord, tutorials) maintain its momentum.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It solves a universal pain point in computer vision: the need for reliable, reusable utilities. Its model-agnostic design, support for popular frameworks (YOLO, Transformers, MMDetection), and rich annotation/dataset tools make it indispensable for both prototyping and production.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Supervision is a must-have for any Python CV project. It reduces code duplication and accelerates development from data loading to deployment. The library's low-code approach and extensive documentation lower the barrier for entry.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward modular, composable CV toolkits. Developers increasingly expect plug-and-play components that work across models and frameworks. Supervision's success indicates that the community values interoperability and ease of use over monolithic solutions.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Computer vision engineers, ML practitioners, data scientists, and hobbyists building applications with object detection, segmentation, tracking, or video analysis.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Heavy reliance on Roboflow ecosystem (API keys for some features) may deter users seeking fully open-source workflows. The project's breadth could lead to maintenance challenges as it grows.

llmSummary.oneLiner: The de facto open-source library for computer vision and image processing, now integrating deep learning capabilities.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Provides a comprehensive, optimized set of tools for real-time computer vision tasks, from basic image manipulation to advanced deep learning inference, eliminating the need to build vision pipelines from scratch.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 88,427 stars and a sustained 222-star daily delta over the past three days, OpenCV continues to attract massive attention. The acceleration ratio of 1 indicates steady, organic growth rather than a sudden spike. This consistent velocity reflects its essential role in AI and robotics workflows.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent commits and a release within 30 days show active maintenance, but no specific event explains the current momentum.
llmSummary.whyTrending: OpenCV remains the foundational library for computer vision in AI applications, from autonomous vehicles to medical imaging. Its deep learning module and Python bindings make it accessible to a broad developer audience, driving continuous adoption.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: OpenCV is a must-know library for any developer working on vision-based AI. Its extensive documentation, community, and integration with frameworks like TensorFlow and PyTorch make it a safe, high-ROI investment.
llmSummary.developerInsight: For AI developers, OpenCV signals the maturation of computer vision as a commodity capability. The library's sustained popularity indicates that vision is becoming a standard component in AI systems, not a niche specialty.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Computer vision engineers, robotics developers, AI researchers, and any developer building applications that require image or video analysis.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The large codebase and broad scope can be overwhelming for newcomers. Some advanced features may lag behind cutting-edge research, and performance optimization requires careful use of C++ APIs.

llmSummary.oneLiner: An open-source AI agent platform for content marketing and monetization across 14+ social platforms, targeting solo entrepreneurs and creators.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Creators and small businesses waste time manually publishing, engaging, and monetizing content across multiple platforms. AiToEarn automates the entire workflow with AI agents, from content creation to cross-platform distribution and performance-based monetization.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo gained 873 stars in the last 24 hours (309/day average) with a sudden breakout pattern, reaching 19,663 total stars. The acceleration ratio is 1 (low), but the rule-based trend type is 'sudden_breakout', indicating a sharp increase in attention. This is likely driven by the recent v2.4 release (May 21, 2026) adding new AI models and enhanced batch generation, combined with growing interest in AI-powered content automation tools.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The latest release v2.4.0 (May 21, 2026) introduced support for HappyHorse 1.0 and Seedance 2.0 for draft generation, enhanced batch video/image generation, multi-model selection, and a new UI. The README was also updated to highlight these features, which likely triggered the star surge.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It combines two hot trends: AI agents and content creator economy. The project offers a complete, open-source solution for content automation and monetization, with a clear value proposition for solo entrepreneurs and small teams. The recent release and active community (3k forks) amplify its visibility.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This project demonstrates how to build a multi-platform content automation tool using AI agents. Developers can learn from its architecture (MCP protocol support, Docker deployment, Electron client) and consider integrating similar agent-based workflows into their own tools.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The rise of AI-powered content marketing agents signals a shift toward autonomous content operations. Developers should explore MCP protocol integration and agent-based automation for social media management, as this could become a standard workflow for creator tools.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Solo entrepreneurs (OPC), content creators, small businesses, and marketing teams who want to automate content publishing, engagement, and monetization across multiple social platforms.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project relies on third-party platform APIs (Douyin, Xiaohongshu, etc.) which may change or restrict access. The monetization model (CPS/CPE/CPM) depends on advertiser demand and platform policies. Also, the rapid star growth may be inflated by marketing or bot activity, though the commit history and release cadence suggest genuine development.

modelDemoSignals.title: OmniGameArena: A Unified UE5 Benchmark for VLM Game Agents with Improvement Dynamics
modelDemoSignals.description: Vision-language model (VLM) agents are increasingly deployed in interactive game environments. Yet game benchmarks for VLM agents typically report a single first-attempt score per (agent, game) pair, focus on single-agent Solo play, and lack unified protocols for evaluating heterogeneous agent classes (commercial VLMs, open-weight VLMs, and specialized game policies) on the same footing. We address these gaps with OmniGameArena, a real-time benchmark of twelve newly built Unreal Engine 5 games s
modelDemoSignals.summary: Vision-language model (VLM) agents are increasingly deployed in interactive game environments. Yet game benchmarks for VLM agents typically report a single first-attempt score per (agent, game) pair, focus on single-agent Solo play, and lack unified protocols for evaluating heterogeneous agent classes (commercial VLMs, open-weight VLMs, and specialized game policies) on the same footing. We address these gaps with OmniGameArena, a real-time benchmark of twelve newly built Unreal Engine 5 games spanning Solo (7), PvP (3), and Coop (2) with unified action interfaces, and the Improvement Dynamics Curve (IDC), an agentic-reflection harness in which a tool-using reflector LLM autonomously refines a bounded skill prompt across multiple rounds. Beyond cold-start leaderboard scores, IDC exposes two additional observables for each (agent, game) pair: how the score evolves across reflection rounds, and how the learned skill behaves on held-out task variants. We report these observables for twelve VLM agents on the cold-start leaderboard and four top agents under IDC.
modelDemoSignals.author: Mingxian Lin, Shengju Qian, Yuqi Liu, Yi-Hua Huang, Yiyu Wang, Wei Huang, Yitang Li, Fan Zhang, Zeyu Hu, Lingting Zhu, Xin Wang, Xiaojuan Qi
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, agent, benchmark, agentic

modelDemoSignals.title: An Agency-Transferring Model-Free Policy Enhancement Technique
modelDemoSignals.description: Training reinforcement learning (RL) policies from scratch is costly: it requires careful reward and environment design, extensive tuning, and substantial computation. modelDemoSignals.summary: Training reinforcement learning (RL) policies from scratch is costly: it requires careful reward and environment design, extensive tuning, and substantial computation. Yet many control problems already have a functional but suboptimal policy available as a baseline. This paper proposes a method for embedding such a baseline into the RL training process, simultaneously improving training efficiency relative to from-scratch methods and producing a learning policy that outperforms the baseline. At each step, the method arbitrates between the baseline policy and a trainable learning policy, initially relying strongly on the baseline policy and then progressively transferring agency to the learning policy. By the end of training, the learning policy is a standalone neural network that operates without baseline policy support. The paper formalizes what it means for the baseline policy to be functional: under this policy, the agent reaches a goal set and remains there with high probability. The proposed arbitration mechanism is designed to exploit this property during training, yielding high goal-reaching rates right from the beginning of training. A theoretical analysis provides a formal interpretation of this behavior under stated assumptions and extends it to the final baseline-free regime, where explicit lower bounds are derived for the goal-reaching probability of the standalone learning policy. Empirical results on continuous-control benchmarks show that the proposed method achieves returns that match or exceed those of competitive approaches, while maintaining the highest goal-reaching rates throughout training among the compared methods -- including in the final stage, where the learning policy operates without any baseline support.
modelDemoSignals.author: Anton Bolychev, Georgiy Malaniya, Sinan Ibrahim, Pavel Osinenko
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent, embedding, benchmark

modelDemoSignals.title: Causally Evaluating the Learnability of Formal Language Tasks
modelDemoSignals.description: Language models, as multi-task learners, acquire a wide range of abilities during training. A fundamental question is how much task-specific data is needed to learn a given task. Answering this for natural language is difficult: tasks are hard to delineate and can confound one another. modelDemoSignals.summary: Language models, as multi-task learners, acquire a wide range of abilities during training. A fundamental question is how much task-specific data is needed to learn a given task. Answering this for natural language is difficult: tasks are hard to delineate and can confound one another. To rigorously investigate the relationship between data frequency and learnability, we turn to a controlled setting using formal languages induced from probabilistic finite automata. These serve as a methodological testbed to demonstrate that standard correlational evaluation practices are inherently flawed. To enable causal analysis, we introduce the binning semiring, an algebraic object that lets us control how often a targeted property occurs in a sampled corpus. We formulate the experimental pipeline as a causal graphical model and derive decomposed Kullback-Leibler divergence metrics to measure the learnability of specific sub-tasks. Our experiments show that evaluating learnability without causal intervention leads to incorrect conclusions due to confounders in correlational analysis, and serve as a warning about correlational pitfalls in natural-language settings.
modelDemoSignals.author: Vésteinn Snæbjarnarson, Anej Svete, Josef Valvoda, Reda Boumasmoud, Brian DuSell, Ryan Cotterell

modelDemoSignals.title: Rethinking the Divergence Regularization in LLM RL
modelDemoSignals.description: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a key component of post-training large language models (LLMs). In practice, LLM RL is often off-policy because of training-inference mismatch and policy staleness, making trust-region control essential for stable optimization. modelDemoSignals.summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a key component of post-training large language models (LLMs). In practice, LLM RL is often off-policy because of training-inference mismatch and policy staleness, making trust-region control essential for stable optimization. Mainstream methods such as PPO and GRPO approximate this control with a ratio-clipping mechanism, but the importance ratio can be a poor proxy for distributional shift in long-tailed vocabularies. Recent work such as DPPO addresses this mismatch by replacing ratio-based clipping with a divergence-based mask, yielding a trust region defined by the sampled token's absolute probability shift. However, DPPO still relies on a hard mask: once a token crosses the trust-region boundary in a harmful direction, its gradient is discarded rather than corrected. To address this, we propose Divergence Regularized Policy Optimization (DRPO), which replaces the hard mask with a smooth advantage-weighted quadratic regularizer on policy shift. DRPO preserves the same trust-region geometry as DPPO while inducing bounded, continuous gradient weights that attenuate diverging updates and provide corrective signals beyond the boundary. Experiments across model scales, architectures, and precision settings show that DRPO improves the stability and efficiency of LLM RL training.
modelDemoSignals.author: Jiarui Yao, Xiangxin Zhou, Penghui Qi, Wee Sun Lee, Liefeng Bo, Tianyu Pang
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm

modelDemoSignals.title: PTL-Diffusion: Manifold-Aware Diffusion with Periodic Terminal Laws
modelDemoSignals.description: Standard diffusion models typically use a single time-homogeneous Gaussian terminal distribution as the reference law for generation. While this choice is analytically convenient and empirically powerful, it provides little explicit structure for data concentrated near low-dimensional manifolds, where different regions of the data distribution may correspond to distinct local geometric or semantic factors. modelDemoSignals.summary: Standard diffusion models typically use a single time-homogeneous Gaussian terminal distribution as the reference law for generation. While this choice is analytically convenient and empirically powerful, it provides little explicit structure for data concentrated near low-dimensional manifolds, where different regions of the data distribution may correspond to distinct local geometric or semantic factors. As a result, the reverse model must recover manifold-level structure almost entirely from an unstructured terminal reference distribution. We propose PTL-Diffusion, a proof-of-concept diffusion framework whose forward noising process converges to a nonconstant periodic family of Gaussian terminal laws rather than to a single invariant law. Unlike a phase-conditioned DDPM, where phase information only enters the denoising network while the forward process remains unchanged, PTL-Diffusion embeds phase structure directly into the forward noising dynamics. The proposed construction remains close to standard denoising diffusion models: for a periodically forced Ornstein--Uhlenbeck-type forward process, we derive closed-form forward marginals, the limiting periodic Gaussian terminal family, and explicit Gaussian reverse posteriors, enabling standard noise-prediction training. We also introduce an invariant-average regularization term coupling the phase-conditioned reverse dynamics through the averaged periodic reference law. Experiments on torus and cylinder point-cloud benchmarks and the Olivetti face dataset show that PTL-Diffusion improves manifold-level distributional matching over matched DDPM baselines, reducing phase-conditioned errors, feature-space covariance errors, and nearest-neighbour manifold distances. These results suggest structured terminal reference laws as a promising direction, while motivating more expressive phase constructions and larger-scale evaluations.

aihotHighlights.title: 腾讯混元发布UniRL：统一多模态强化学习基础设施
aihotHighlights.summary: 腾讯混元推出UniRL，一个支持统一多模态模型的强化学习基础设施，并发布两个新算法DRPO和Flow-DPPO。UniRL通过单个后训练循环（生成→评分→优势→更新→同步）覆盖扩散/流匹配模型、LLM/VLM及统一多模态模型（如Hunyuan-Image 3和Bagel）。模型与算法作为独立轴，可实现模型×算法的组合覆盖。框架支持可插拔rollout引擎（训练侧/SGLang/vLLM-Omni）、FSDP2分片和三种部署模式。FlowDPPO针对流/扩散模型引入基于精确散度的信任域策略优化；DRPO为LLM RL提供平滑的优势加权二次正则化方法。代码已开源。

aihotHighlights.title: 火山引擎TRAE Work企业版正式上线，面向全员提供AI办公平台
aihotHighlights.summary: 火山引擎将TRAE Solo品牌升级为TRAE Work企业版，发布面向企业的AI办公平台。平台提供Work和Code两种模式：Work模式面向产品、运营、市场等非技术岗位，支持上传.pptx、.xlsx、图片等多种格式混合输入直接输出PPT或文档，支持语音讨论自动整理结构化纪要，支持按天或按周自动运行的数据整理和报告生成；Code模式面向开发者和业务同学，可用自然语言描述需求直接生成页面或小应用。桌面端、网页端和移动端多端同步。企业后台可统一配置模型、用量限额、上传内部文档，沙箱机制隔离执行，支持命令黑名单、MCP白名单、内容安全策略，关键操作可审计。同时发起TRAE Enterprise Fellow招募计划。

aihotHighlights.title: Kimi 预测全部104场世界杯比赛：德国或被低估
aihotHighlights.summary: Kimi 利用 Agent Swarm 系统并行协调300个子智能体，分析战术、球员状态、伤病、赛程、天气、赔率等因素，预测2026年美加墨世界杯全部104场比赛，并发布每轮赛前预测和赛后回顾。模型层融合了 Elo/FIFA 强度、Poisson 进球分布、xG/xT 指标、蒙特卡洛模拟等方法。预测结果显示西班牙和法国为头号热门，但德国夺冠概率可能被市场低估：模型基线估计约11.0%，校准估计约11.3%，而部分市场隐含概率仅约7.4%，正向偏差约+3.6个百分点。该判断基于多分析链交叉验证，可能源于对德国近两届小组出局的近因偏差以及纳格尔斯曼高位压迫体系与穆西亚拉/维尔茨新创造轴的复苏信号。

aihotHighlights.title: 可灵AI×候鸟300发起AI主义新浪潮AIGC影像大赛
aihotHighlights.summary: 可灵AI与候鸟300联合发起AIGC影像大赛。线下大赛6月16-26日在阿那亚海边举办，评审包括李少红、陈明昊、焦雄屏等影视与AI专家及美院教授，设10万奖金+超200万灵感值，结果6月27日晚揭晓。线上大赛6月22日前投稿可角逐"最受关注大奖"，获奖者受邀参加6月27日颁奖。参赛要求：视频≥50%由可灵AI生成，官网投稿填表并带话题分享。

aihotHighlights.title: 一个Agent如何通过链式调用两个HuggingFace Space构建3D巴黎画廊
aihotHighlights.summary: 一个编码Agent调用HuggingFace上的两个Space，从零构建了展示巴黎地标3D高斯散点图的交互式画廊。Agent先用ideogram-ai/ideogram4生成每个纪念碑的黑色背景图像，再通过VAST-AI/TripoSplat从单张图像重建3D高斯散点（.ply），自动完成坐标系校正、取景、压缩为.ksplat（体积缩小约3倍），并构建基于Three.js的滚动切换、拖拽旋转查看器，最终部署为静态Space。整个过程无需客户端库，每个Space通过`agents.md`暴露可调用API。

aihotHighlights.title: 仅凭一份文档，Qwen3.7-Max 从 0 交付双端应用
aihotHighlights.summary: 在无设计稿和后端代码的条件下，Qwen3.7-Max 仅凭一份约 15 万字的产品调研文档，于隔离环境中全自动完成移动端与 Web 端两套真实应用从 0 到 1 交付，单端耗时约 4 小时，中途无人工接管。模型不具备图像理解能力，通过像素坐标反推布局约束实现界面还原。实验采用"分阶段注入约束→逐层验收→带错纠正"的闭环控制系统：任务拆分为规划、架构、编码等阶段，验收覆盖静态检查、编译自检（0 error）、路由完整性（Web 端 34 条路由全部可达）、功能扫描及真机冷启动冒烟。失败时错误文本自动注入下一轮重试，使模型数小时内收敛。移动端产出可安装 APK，Web 端 typecheck 与构建均通过。

aihotHighlights.title: NeuroBait：微调AI助手，为ADHD大脑点燃多巴胺火花
aihotHighlights.summary: NeuroBait是基于Google gemma-3-12b-it微调的AI对话助手，旨在帮助ADHD患者克服"知道该做什么但无法开始"的执行功能障碍。采用16-bit LoRA（r=16， alpha=16）在Unsloth上训练3个epoch，学习率2e-4，最大序列长度2048，使用单张H100 80GB GPU。数据集为基于真实ADHD场景手工合成的少量数据。部署于Hugging Face Space（ZeroGPU），使用Gradio和标准transformers+peft，运行时以4-bit NF4加载基础模型并应用LoRA适配器。NeuroBait不生成待办清单，而是根据用户上下文给出3-6句温暖流畅的回复，引导用户找到一件微小可立即执行的动作，从而激发多巴胺、降低启动阻力。

aihotHighlights.title: NVIDIA cuTile Python 教程：在 Colab 中构建用于向量加法、矩阵加法和矩阵乘法的 Tiled GPU 内核
aihotHighlights.summary: 该教程基于 NVIDIA cuTile Python 实现了分块 GPU 内核编程工作流，在 Colab 环境中配置 GPU、驱动、CUDA 及 cuTile 可用性后，分别构建了 tiled 向量加法、矩阵加法和矩阵乘法核函数，并以 PyTorch 作为回退保持 notebook 可执行。每一步均通过 PyTorch 验证结果正确性，并基准测试了各阶段的中位运行时间。

aihotHighlights.summary: 硅基流动宣布，通过V4-Pro（质量）与V4-Flash（速度）两行配置，即可在终端获得DeepSeek V4的最佳性价比组合。专为DeepSeek V4构建的终端编码智能体CodeWhale现已内置SiliconFlow。CodeWhale具备流式推理（显示思考过程）、自动路由（根据任务复杂度切换模型与思考深度）、零漂移（通过书面宪法为每轮排序权威，保持V4定向）以及自我改进（V4协助编写框架，框架提升后每个会话更强大）等特性。

aihotHighlights.title: AI 编程独角兽 Cursor 欧洲总部落子伦敦，SpaceX 手握 600 亿美元收购选择权
aihotHighlights.summary: Cursor 将欧洲总部设在伦敦，计划招聘约 200 名员工，并在巴黎、慕尼黑等地开设小型办事处。SpaceX 拥有以 600 亿美元收购 Cursor 的选择权，或支付 100 亿美元开展全新合作。Cursor 目前 B2B 年化营收约 26 亿美元，客户包括英国航空、英国石油、诺基亚等。公司强调数据留存欧洲本地以满足监管合规，其平台支持用户用自然语言生成代码，主打模型中立，竞争对手包括 GitHub Copilot、OpenAI 和谷歌的同类产品。

aihotHighlights.title: 小米 MiMo 与 TileRT 联合发布 UltraSpeed 模式，1T 模型输出突破 1000 tokens/s
aihotHighlights.summary: 小米 MiMo 与 TileRT 联合发布 MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed 模式，使 1T 参数旗舰模型输出速度首次突破 1000 tokens/s。模型侧采用 FP4 混合量化（仅量化 MoE Expert）与 DFlash 块级 masked 并行推测解码（coding 场景平均接受长度 6.30 tokens）；系统侧 TileRT 引入常驻内核引擎与异构流水线协作。API 限时开放（2026 年 6 月 9 日至 23 日），定价为 MiMo-V2.5-Pro 的 3 倍，速度提升约 10 倍。FP4 权重与 DFlash 模型 checkpoint 已开源至 HuggingFace。
aihotHighlights.description: 小米 MiMo 与 TileRT 联合发布 MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed 模式，使 1T 参数旗舰模型输出速度首次突破 1000 tokens/s。模型侧采用 FP4 混合量化（仅量化 MoE Expert）与 DFlash 块级 masked 并行推测解码（coding 场景平均接受长度 6.30 tokens）；系统侧 TileRT 引入常驻内核引擎与异构流水线协作。API 限时开放（2026 年 6 月 9 日至 23 日），定价为 MiMo-V2.5-Pro 的 3 倍，速度提升约 10 倍。FP4 权重与 DFlash 模型 checkpoint 已开源至 HuggingFace。
aihotHighlights.recommendedReason: 小米 MiMo 与 TileRT 联合发布 MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed 模式，使 1T 参数旗舰模型输出速度首次突破 1000 tokens/s。模型侧采用 FP4 混合量化（仅量化 MoE Expert）与 DFlash 块级 masked 并行推测解码（coding 场景平均接受长度 6.30 tokens）；系统侧 TileRT 引入常驻内核引擎与异构流水线协作。API 限时开放（2026 年 6 月 9 日至 23 日），定价为 MiMo-V2.5-Pro 的 3 倍，速度提升约 10 倍。FP4 权重与 DFlash 模型 checkpoint 已开源至 HuggingFace。

aihotHighlights.title: OpenAI 秘密提交 IPO 申请，奥特曼旗下 Tools for Humanity 裁员
aihotHighlights.summary: OpenAI 近日秘密提交 IPO 申请。其 CEO 山姆·奥特曼旗下的 Tools for Humanity 公司正裁员，该公司因虹膜扫描项目 World 及加密货币 Worldcoin 知名，投后估值 25 亿美元，获 Andreessen Horowitz 等投资。因营收困境，公司缩减规模。海外监管方面，肯尼亚以隐私和金融风险为由叫停运营，韩国因违反隐私法规罚款 83 万美元。

multiSourceItems.title: 腾讯混元发布UniRL：统一多模态强化学习基础设施
multiSourceItems.summary: 腾讯混元推出UniRL，一个支持统一多模态模型的强化学习基础设施，并发布两个新算法DRPO和Flow-DPPO。UniRL通过单个后训练循环（生成→评分→优势→更新→同步）覆盖扩散/流匹配模型、LLM/VLM及统一多模态模型（如Hunyuan-Image 3和Bagel）。模型与算法作为独立轴，可实现模型×算法的组合覆盖。框架支持可插拔rollout引擎（训练侧/SGLang/vLLM-Omni）、FSDP2分片和三种部署模式。FlowDPPO针对流/扩散模型引入基于精确散度的信任域策略优化；DRPO为LLM RL提供平滑的优势加权二次正则化方法。代码已开源。

multiSourceItems.title: 火山引擎TRAE Work企业版正式上线，面向全员提供AI办公平台
multiSourceItems.summary: 火山引擎将TRAE Solo品牌升级为TRAE Work企业版，发布面向企业的AI办公平台。平台提供Work和Code两种模式：Work模式面向产品、运营、市场等非技术岗位，支持上传.pptx、.xlsx、图片等多种格式混合输入直接输出PPT或文档，支持语音讨论自动整理结构化纪要，支持按天或按周自动运行的数据整理和报告生成；Code模式面向开发者和业务同学，可用自然语言描述需求直接生成页面或小应用。桌面端、网页端和移动端多端同步。企业后台可统一配置模型、用量限额、上传内部文档，沙箱机制隔离执行，支持命令黑名单、MCP白名单、内容安全策略，关键操作可审计。同时发起TRAE Enterprise Fellow招募计划。

multiSourceItems.title: Kimi 预测全部104场世界杯比赛：德国或被低估
multiSourceItems.summary: Kimi 利用 Agent Swarm 系统并行协调300个子智能体，分析战术、球员状态、伤病、赛程、天气、赔率等因素，预测2026年美加墨世界杯全部104场比赛，并发布每轮赛前预测和赛后回顾。模型层融合了 Elo/FIFA 强度、Poisson 进球分布、xG/xT 指标、蒙特卡洛模拟等方法。预测结果显示西班牙和法国为头号热门，但德国夺冠概率可能被市场低估：模型基线估计约11.0%，校准估计约11.3%，而部分市场隐含概率仅约7.4%，正向偏差约+3.6个百分点。该判断基于多分析链交叉验证，可能源于对德国近两届小组出局的近因偏差以及纳格尔斯曼高位压迫体系与穆西亚拉/维尔茨新创造轴的复苏信号。

multiSourceItems.title: 可灵AI×候鸟300发起AI主义新浪潮AIGC影像大赛
multiSourceItems.summary: 可灵AI与候鸟300联合发起AIGC影像大赛。线下大赛6月16-26日在阿那亚海边举办，评审包括李少红、陈明昊、焦雄屏等影视与AI专家及美院教授，设10万奖金+超200万灵感值，结果6月27日晚揭晓。线上大赛6月22日前投稿可角逐"最受关注大奖"，获奖者受邀参加6月27日颁奖。参赛要求：视频≥50%由可灵AI生成，官网投稿填表并带话题分享。

multiSourceItems.title: 一个Agent如何通过链式调用两个HuggingFace Space构建3D巴黎画廊
multiSourceItems.summary: 一个编码Agent调用HuggingFace上的两个Space，从零构建了展示巴黎地标3D高斯散点图的交互式画廊。Agent先用ideogram-ai/ideogram4生成每个纪念碑的黑色背景图像，再通过VAST-AI/TripoSplat从单张图像重建3D高斯散点（.ply），自动完成坐标系校正、取景、压缩为.ksplat（体积缩小约3倍），并构建基于Three.js的滚动切换、拖拽旋转查看器，最终部署为静态Space。整个过程无需客户端库，每个Space通过`agents.md`暴露可调用API。

multiSourceItems.title: 仅凭一份文档，Qwen3.7-Max 从 0 交付双端应用
multiSourceItems.summary: 在无设计稿和后端代码的条件下，Qwen3.7-Max 仅凭一份约 15 万字的产品调研文档，于隔离环境中全自动完成移动端与 Web 端两套真实应用从 0 到 1 交付，单端耗时约 4 小时，中途无人工接管。模型不具备图像理解能力，通过像素坐标反推布局约束实现界面还原。实验采用"分阶段注入约束→逐层验收→带错纠正"的闭环控制系统：任务拆分为规划、架构、编码等阶段，验收覆盖静态检查、编译自检（0 error）、路由完整性（Web 端 34 条路由全部可达）、功能扫描及真机冷启动冒烟。失败时错误文本自动注入下一轮重试，使模型数小时内收敛。移动端产出可安装 APK，Web 端 typecheck 与构建均通过。

multiSourceItems.title: NeuroBait：微调AI助手，为ADHD大脑点燃多巴胺火花
multiSourceItems.summary: NeuroBait是基于Google gemma-3-12b-it微调的AI对话助手，旨在帮助ADHD患者克服"知道该做什么但无法开始"的执行功能障碍。采用16-bit LoRA（r=16， alpha=16）在Unsloth上训练3个epoch，学习率2e-4，最大序列长度2048，使用单张H100 80GB GPU。数据集为基于真实ADHD场景手工合成的少量数据。部署于Hugging Face Space（ZeroGPU），使用Gradio和标准transformers+peft，运行时以4-bit NF4加载基础模型并应用LoRA适配器。NeuroBait不生成待办清单，而是根据用户上下文给出3-6句温暖流畅的回复，引导用户找到一件微小可立即执行的动作，从而激发多巴胺、降低启动阻力。

multiSourceItems.title: NVIDIA cuTile Python 教程：在 Colab 中构建用于向量加法、矩阵加法和矩阵乘法的 Tiled GPU 内核
multiSourceItems.summary: 该教程基于 NVIDIA cuTile Python 实现了分块 GPU 内核编程工作流，在 Colab 环境中配置 GPU、驱动、CUDA 及 cuTile 可用性后，分别构建了 tiled 向量加法、矩阵加法和矩阵乘法核函数，并以 PyTorch 作为回退保持 notebook 可执行。每一步均通过 PyTorch 验证结果正确性，并基准测试了各阶段的中位运行时间。

multiSourceItems.title: 硅基流动联合CodeWhale推出DeepSeek V4终端最佳性价比组合
multiSourceItems.summary: 硅基流动宣布，通过V4-Pro（质量）与V4-Flash（速度）两行配置，即可在终端获得DeepSeek V4的最佳性价比组合。专为DeepSeek V4构建的终端编码智能体CodeWhale现已内置SiliconFlow。CodeWhale具备流式推理（显示思考过程）、自动路由（根据任务复杂度切换模型与思考深度）、零漂移（通过书面宪法为每轮排序权威，保持V4定向）以及自我改进（V4协助编写框架，框架提升后每个会话更强大）等特性。

multiSourceItems.title: AI 编程独角兽 Cursor 欧洲总部落子伦敦，SpaceX 手握 600 亿美元收购选择权
multiSourceItems.summary: Cursor 将欧洲总部设在伦敦，计划招聘约 200 名员工，并在巴黎、慕尼黑等地开设小型办事处。SpaceX 拥有以 600 亿美元收购 Cursor 的选择权，或支付 100 亿美元开展全新合作。Cursor 目前 B2B 年化营收约 26 亿美元，客户包括英国航空、英国石油、诺基亚等。公司强调数据留存欧洲本地以满足监管合规，其平台支持用户用自然语言生成代码，主打模型中立，竞争对手包括 GitHub Copilot、OpenAI 和谷歌的同类产品。

multiSourceItems.title: 小米 MiMo 与 TileRT 联合发布 UltraSpeed 模式，1T 模型输出突破 1000 tokens/s
multiSourceItems.summary: 小米 MiMo 与 TileRT 联合发布 MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed 模式，使 1T 参数旗舰模型输出速度首次突破 1000 tokens/s。模型侧采用 FP4 混合量化（仅量化 MoE Expert）与 DFlash 块级 masked 并行推测解码（coding 场景平均接受长度 6.30 tokens）；系统侧 TileRT 引入常驻内核引擎与异构流水线协作。API 限时开放（2026 年 6 月 9 日至 23 日），定价为 MiMo-V2.5-Pro 的 3 倍，速度提升约 10 倍。FP4 权重与 DFlash 模型 checkpoint 已开源至 HuggingFace。
multiSourceItems.description: 小米 MiMo 与 TileRT 联合发布 MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed 模式，使 1T 参数旗舰模型输出速度首次突破 1000 tokens/s。模型侧采用 FP4 混合量化（仅量化 MoE Expert）与 DFlash 块级 masked 并行推测解码（coding 场景平均接受长度 6.30 tokens）；系统侧 TileRT 引入常驻内核引擎与异构流水线协作。API 限时开放（2026 年 6 月 9 日至 23 日），定价为 MiMo-V2.5-Pro 的 3 倍，速度提升约 10 倍。FP4 权重与 DFlash 模型 checkpoint 已开源至 HuggingFace。

multiSourceItems.title: OpenAI 秘密提交 IPO 申请，奥特曼旗下 Tools for Humanity 裁员
multiSourceItems.summary: OpenAI 近日秘密提交 IPO 申请。其 CEO 山姆·奥特曼旗下的 Tools for Humanity 公司正裁员，该公司因虹膜扫描项目 World 及加密货币 Worldcoin 知名，投后估值 25 亿美元，获 Andreessen Horowitz 等投资。因营收困境，公司缩减规模。海外监管方面，肯尼亚以隐私和金融风险为由叫停运营，韩国因违反隐私法规罚款 83 万美元。

multiSourceItems.title: Elon Musk 详解 SpaceX AI1 轨道 AI 数据中心卫星方案
multiSourceItems.summary: Elon Musk 首次详细解释 SpaceX 的 AI1 轨道 AI 数据中心卫星：峰值功率 150 kW，持续计算功率约 120 kW，相当于一个 NVIDIA GB300 机架；太阳能板效率 250 W/m2；双面散热器排热 1，400 W/m2。通过激光链路实现约 1 Tbps 互联，低轨 600-800 km 高度往返延迟 6-8 ms。由 Starship 发射，计划部署多达百万颗卫星，2027 年底前实现量产。近地目标为吉瓦级轨道 AI 算力，长期向太瓦级推进。

multiSourceItems.title: 开源工具 Tokei：在菜单栏实时监控 AI coding agent 的 token 用量与成本
multiSourceItems.summary: Berry Xia 推荐开源工具 Tokei，这是一个 macOS 菜单栏小工具，只读本地日志、零网络调用，30 秒自动刷新，实时监控 Claude Code、Grok CLI、Aider、OpenCode 等 8 个主流 AI coding agent 的 token 用量、实时成本与性能数据，并附每日图表、周热力图和年度 Wrapped。支持私人 Git 多设备同步、价格表本地覆盖，闲置过久会提醒休息。引用推文指出，token 消耗情况可作为深度使用 AI 的面试亮点，数据全程私有，零侵入，无需任何认证。

multiSourceItems.summary: 工信部、国资委6月8日联合发布通知，目标到2026年底，人形机器人等重点产品在代表性场景完成应用验证并开启常态部署，形成百个以上高价值场景，万台级规模落地。要求各省级地区选取不少于20个场景单元（覆盖两类领域），央企不少于10个。围绕打造实景实训空间、组建创新应用联合体、攻关作业技能、加强验证部署、强化要素保障、凝练经验等六大任务展开，鼓励"人形机器人即服务"等商业创新。

multiSourceItems.title: FrontierCode 基准测试：AI 编程评估新标准--维护者审核通过率最高仅 13.4%
multiSourceItems.summary: Cognition 发布 FrontierCode 基准测试，重新定义 AI 编程评估：由 20 多位顶级开源维护者手工制作 150 个任务（每个耗时 40+ 小时），依据 3000 多条规则判断维护者是否愿意合并代码。该基准指出 SWE-Bench 等超半数通过测试的代码实为不可维护的垃圾。结果中 Claude Opus 4.8 在最高难度档获 13.4%，GPT-5.5 为 6.3%，其余模型 1%-5%。这意味着即便最强模型，近九成代码仍无法通过有经验维护者审核。

multiSourceItems.title: GitHub 122K⭐的Skills推出新技能「Teach」：把工作目录变有状态学习空间
multiSourceItems.summary: GitHub 122K⭐的Skills仓库推出新技能Teach，可将当前工作目录变为有状态学习空间。设计理念从Knowledge（概念事实）→Skills（动手操作）→Wisdom（真实判断）。工作区以文件即学习状态：MISSION.md定目标、lessons/提供课程、learning-records/记录已会内容、reference/生成速查手册。五个关键机制：Mission定方向、ZPD根据记录调整难度、Lesson一课一事+即时反馈、Learning Record记"会了什么"而非"讲了什么"、Reference形成长期可回看手册。可通过npx skills add mattpocock/skills --skill teach安装，适用于从魔方到软件基础等学习场景。

multiSourceItems.title: 奥尔特曼宣布 OpenAI 进入第三发展阶段：让 AI 普及、易用且安全
multiSourceItems.summary: 本周一，OpenAI CEO 奥尔特曼与首席科学家帕霍茨基联合发文，宣布公司进入第三发展阶段，目标让 AI 普及、易用且安全。此前第一阶段聚焦通用人工智能技术研发，第二阶段面向全球推出产品。第三阶段三大核心目标是打造自动化人工智能研究员、推动经济提速、为每人配备专属通用人工智能。二人强调智能系统须坚守安全底线，呼吁成立国际机构应对 AI 风险，必要时可暂缓前沿模型研发。同日，OpenAI 秘密提交 IPO 申请，但上市仍需较长时间。上周 Anthropic 研究人员也建议适当放缓前沿 AI 研发。

multiSourceItems.title: Claude 支持 Apple Foundation Models 框架，推出新 Swift 包
multiSourceItems.summary: Anthropic 通过一个新 Swift 包，让 Apple 开发者能在 Foundation Models 框架中直接调用 Claude。开发者用 Apple 框架以原生 Swift 方式，仅需三行代码即可通过引导生成返回类型化 Swift 值。当请求涉及多步推理、代码生成、联网搜索或数据分析时，Claude 可接手处理，并将流式响应传回同一视图。该支持可在 iOS 27、iPadOS 27、macOS 27、visionOS 27 和 watchOS 27 上使用，开发者需添加包并配置 Anthropic API 密钥。

multiSourceItems.title: OmniGameArena: A Unified UE5 Benchmark for VLM Game Agents with Improvement Dynamics
multiSourceItems.description: Vision-language model (VLM) agents are increasingly deployed in interactive game environments. Yet game benchmarks for VLM agents typically report a single first-attempt score per (agent, game) pair, focus on single-agent Solo play, and lack unified protocols for evaluating heterogeneous agent classes (commercial VLMs, open-weight VLMs, and specialized game policies) on the same footing. We address these gaps with OmniGameArena, a real-time benchmark of twelve newly built Unreal Engine 5 games s
multiSourceItems.summary: Vision-language model (VLM) agents are increasingly deployed in interactive game environments. Yet game benchmarks for VLM agents typically report a single first-attempt score per (agent, game) pair, focus on single-agent Solo play, and lack unified protocols for evaluating heterogeneous agent classes (commercial VLMs, open-weight VLMs, and specialized game policies) on the same footing. We address these gaps with OmniGameArena, a real-time benchmark of twelve newly built Unreal Engine 5 games spanning Solo (7), PvP (3), and Coop (2) with unified action interfaces, and the Improvement Dynamics Curve (IDC), an agentic-reflection harness in which a tool-using reflector LLM autonomously refines a bounded skill prompt across multiple rounds. Beyond cold-start leaderboard scores, IDC exposes two additional observables for each (agent, game) pair: how the score evolves across reflection rounds, and how the learned skill behaves on held-out task variants. We report these observables for twelve VLM agents on the cold-start leaderboard and four top agents under IDC.
multiSourceItems.author: Mingxian Lin, Shengju Qian, Yuqi Liu, Yi-Hua Huang, Yiyu Wang, Wei Huang, Yitang Li, Fan Zhang, Zeyu Hu, Lingting Zhu, Xin Wang, Xiaojuan Qi
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, agent, benchmark, agentic

multiSourceItems.title: An Agency-Transferring Model-Free Policy Enhancement Technique
multiSourceItems.description: Training reinforcement learning (RL) policies from scratch is costly: it requires careful reward and environment design, extensive tuning, and substantial computation. Yet many control problems already have a functional but suboptimal policy available as a baseline. This paper proposes a method for embedding such a baseline into the RL training process, simultaneously improving training efficiency relative to from-scratch methods and producing a learning policy that outperforms the baseline. At
multiSourceItems.summary: Training reinforcement learning (RL) policies from scratch is costly: it requires careful reward and environment design, extensive tuning, and substantial computation. Yet many control problems already have a functional but suboptimal policy available as a baseline. This paper proposes a method for embedding such a baseline into the RL training process, simultaneously improving training efficiency relative to from-scratch methods and producing a learning policy that outperforms the baseline. At each step, the method arbitrates between the baseline policy and a trainable learning policy, initially relying strongly on the baseline policy and then progressively transferring agency to the learning policy. By the end of training, the learning policy is a standalone neural network that operates without baseline policy support. The paper formalizes what it means for the baseline policy to be functional: under this policy, the agent reaches a goal set and remains there with high probability. The proposed arbitration mechanism is designed to exploit this property during training, yielding high goal-reaching rates right from the beginning of training. A theoretical analysis provides a formal interpretation of this behavior under stated assumptions and extends it to the final baseline-free regime, where explicit lower bounds are derived for the goal-reaching probability of the standalone learning policy. Empirical results on continuous-control benchmarks show that the proposed method achieves returns that match or exceed those of competitive approaches, while maintaining the highest goal-reaching rates throughout training among the compared methods -- including in the final stage, where the learning policy operates without any baseline support.

multiSourceItems.title: Causally Evaluating the Learnability of Formal Language Tasks
multiSourceItems.description: Language models, as multi-task learners, acquire a wide range of abilities during training. A fundamental question is how much task-specific data is needed to learn a given task. Answering this for natural language is difficult: tasks are hard to delineate and can confound one another. These serve as a methodologica
multiSourceItems.summary: Language models, as multi-task learners, acquire a wide range of abilities during training. A fundamental question is how much task-specific data is needed to learn a given task. Answering this for natural language is difficult: tasks are hard to delineate and can confound one another. To rigorously investigate the relationship between data frequency and learnability, we turn to a controlled setting using formal languages induced from probabilistic finite automata. These serve as a methodological testbed to demonstrate that standard correlational evaluation practices are inherently flawed. To enable causal analysis, we introduce the binning semiring, an algebraic object that lets us control how often a targeted property occurs in a sampled corpus. We formulate the experimental pipeline as a causal graphical model and derive decomposed Kullback-Leibler divergence metrics to measure the learnability of specific sub-tasks. Our experiments show that evaluating learnability without causal intervention leads to incorrect conclusions due to confounders in correlational analysis, and serve as a warning about correlational pitfalls in natural-language settings.
multiSourceItems.author: Vésteinn Snæbjarnarson, Anej Svete, Josef Valvoda, Reda Boumasmoud, Brian DuSell, Ryan Cotterell

multiSourceItems.description: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a key component of post-training large language models (LLMs). In practice, LLM RL is often off-policy because of training-inference mismatch and policy staleness, making trust-region control essential for stable optimization. multiSourceItems.summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a key component of post-training large language models (LLMs). In practice, LLM RL is often off-policy because of training-inference mismatch and policy staleness, making trust-region control essential for stable optimization. Mainstream methods such as PPO and GRPO approximate this control with a ratio-clipping mechanism, but the importance ratio can be a poor proxy for distributional shift in long-tailed vocabularies. Recent work such as DPPO addresses this mismatch by replacing ratio-based clipping with a divergence-based mask, yielding a trust region defined by the sampled token's absolute probability shift. However, DPPO still relies on a hard mask: once a token crosses the trust-region boundary in a harmful direction, its gradient is discarded rather than corrected. To address this, we propose Divergence Regularized Policy Optimization (DRPO), which replaces the hard mask with a smooth advantage-weighted quadratic regularizer on policy shift. DRPO preserves the same trust-region geometry as DPPO while inducing bounded, continuous gradient weights that attenuate diverging updates and provide corrective signals beyond the boundary. Experiments across model scales, architectures, and precision settings show that DRPO improves the stability and efficiency of LLM RL training.
multiSourceItems.author: Jiarui Yao, Xiangxin Zhou, Penghui Qi, Wee Sun Lee, Liefeng Bo, Tianyu Pang
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm

multiSourceItems.title: PTL-Diffusion: Manifold-Aware Diffusion with Periodic Terminal Laws
multiSourceItems.description: Standard diffusion models typically use a single time-homogeneous Gaussian terminal distribution as the reference law for generation. While this choice is analytically convenient and empirically powerful, it provides little explicit structure for data concentrated near low-dimensional manifolds, where different regions of the data distribution may correspond to distinct local geometric or semantic factors. multiSourceItems.summary: Standard diffusion models typically use a single time-homogeneous Gaussian terminal distribution as the reference law for generation. While this choice is analytically convenient and empirically powerful, it provides little explicit structure for data concentrated near low-dimensional manifolds, where different regions of the data distribution may correspond to distinct local geometric or semantic factors. As a result, the reverse model must recover manifold-level structure almost entirely from an unstructured terminal reference distribution. We propose PTL-Diffusion, a proof-of-concept diffusion framework whose forward noising process converges to a nonconstant periodic family of Gaussian terminal laws rather than to a single invariant law. Unlike a phase-conditioned DDPM, where phase information only enters the denoising network while the forward process remains unchanged, PTL-Diffusion embeds phase structure directly into the forward noising dynamics. The proposed construction remains close to standard denoising diffusion models: for a periodically forced Ornstein--Uhlenbeck-type forward process, we derive closed-form forward marginals, the limiting periodic Gaussian terminal family, and explicit Gaussian reverse posteriors, enabling standard noise-prediction training. We also introduce an invariant-average regularization term coupling the phase-conditioned reverse dynamics through the averaged periodic reference law. Experiments on torus and cylinder point-cloud benchmarks and the Olivetti face dataset show that PTL-Diffusion improves manifold-level distributional matching over matched DDPM baselines, reducing phase-conditioned errors, feature-space covariance errors, and nearest-neighbour manifold distances. These results suggest structured terminal reference laws as a promising direction, while motivating more expressive phase constructions and larger-scale evaluations.
multiSourceItems.author: Danqi Zhuang, Jisui Huang, Xiaoyue Xi, Andrew Kiggins, Xiaojie Wang, Ke Chen, Yue Wu
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: rag, benchmark, evaluation

multiSourceItems.title: AHA-WAM:Asynchronous Horizon-Adaptive World-Action Modeling with Observation-Guided Context Routing
multiSourceItems.description: World-action models have emerged as a promising paradigm for robot manipulation, jointly modeling visual scene dynamics and actions to inject physical priors into policy learning. However, existing world-action models couple world prediction and action execution at the same temporal resolution, forcing the world branch to model near-term frame variations that are redundant and weakly informative. We posit that strictly binding world prediction and action execution to the same temporal rhythm may
multiSourceItems.summary: World-action models have emerged as a promising paradigm for robot manipulation, jointly modeling visual scene dynamics and actions to inject physical priors into policy learning. However, existing world-action models couple world prediction and action execution at the same temporal resolution, forcing the world branch to model near-term frame variations that are redundant and weakly informative. We posit that strictly binding world prediction and action execution to the same temporal rhythm may underutilize the potential of the video branch for embodied control. Therefore, we propose AHA-WAM, an Asynchronous Horizon-Adaptive World-Action Model built on a dual Diffusion Transformer (DiT) architecture that reorganizes world-action modeling around this temporal asymmetry. AHA-WAM instantiates the video DiT as a low-frequency world planner that maintains rolling key-value memory over past observations and exposes reusable layerwise latent context encoding long-horizon scene evolution, while a high-frequency action DiT executes short action chunks in closed loop by querying this context through layerwise joint attention. To support asynchronous execution, we introduce horizon-adaptive offset training and Observation-Guided Video-Context Routing (OVCR), which together let the action expert exploit long-horizon world context while remaining responsive to real-time execution state without rerunning the video DiT. Experiments on RoboTwin and real-world manipulation tasks show that AHA-WAM achieves state-of-the-art performance without any robot-data pretraining, attaining 92.80% average success on RoboTwin and 78.3% success across 4 real-world tasks, while reaching 24.17 Hz closed-loop control with a 4.59x speedup over Fast-WAM.
multiSourceItems.author: Jisong Cai, Long Ling, Shiwei Chu, Zhongshan Liu, Jiayue Kang, Zhixuan Liang, Wenjie Xu, Yinan Mao, Weinan Zhang, Xiaokang Yang, Ru Ying, Ran Zheng, Yao Mu

multiSourceItems.title: Evaluation Cards: An Interpretive Layer for AI Evaluation Reporting
multiSourceItems.description: AI evaluation results are produced at scale but reported inconsistently across leaderboards, model cards, benchmark papers, and company blogs. The cost is interpretive: readers cannot reliably compare results across sources, identify what a report omits, or trace an aggregate claim to its underlying evidence. Recent efforts address isolated components but leave three gaps: they cover only narrow slices of the evaluation lifecycle and do not compose into a single interpretable record; they specif
multiSourceItems.summary: AI evaluation results are produced at scale but reported inconsistently across leaderboards, model cards, benchmark papers, and company blogs. The cost is interpretive: readers cannot reliably compare results across sources, identify what a report omits, or trace an aggregate claim to its underlying evidence. Recent efforts address isolated components but leave three gaps: they cover only narrow slices of the evaluation lifecycle and do not compose into a single interpretable record; they specify static representations that do not differentiate the questions different stakeholders bring to the same evidence; and they remain proposals on paper, lacking the extraction infrastructure required for adoption at scale. We present \EvalCards{}, an operational reporting layer that composes benchmark metadata, evaluation run data, and model metadata into a unified record. We (1) derive a reporting schema from a structured review of 52 papers and 10 stakeholder interviews, (2) implement four interpretive signals (reproducibility, documentation completeness, provenance and risk, and score comparability), rendered through reader modes calibrated to research and non-research audiences, and (3) deploy a monitoring tool that applies \EvalCards{} across 5,816 models, 635 benchmarks, and 101,843 results, surfacing systematic gaps in current reporting practice.
multiSourceItems.author: Avijit Ghosh, Anka Reuel, Jenny Chim, Wm. Matthew Kennedy, Srishti Yadav, Jennifer Mickel, Yanan Long, Andrew Tran, Anastassia Kornilova, Damian Stachura, Kevin Klyman, Felix Friedrich, Jeba Sania, Max Lamparth, Jan Batzner, Anoop Mishra, Eliya Habba, Yixiong Hao, Nathan Heath, Shalaleh Rismani, Usman Gohar, Andrea Loehr, David Manheim, Ruchira Dhar, Sree Harsha Nelaturu, Aarush Sinha, Leshem Choshen, Drishti Sharma, Ishan Khire, Amit Saha, Subramanyam Sahoo, Michael Hardy, Michael Alexander Riegler, Kabir Manghnani, Michelle Lin, Yanan Jiang, Yilin Huang, Asaf Yehudai, Jessica Ji, Aris Hofmann, Mubashara Akhtar, Nuno Moniz, Yacine Jernite, Stella Biderman, Zeerak Talat, Sanmi Koyejo, Mykel Kochenderfer, Irene Solaiman

multiSourceItems.title: Topological Neural Operators
multiSourceItems.description: We introduce Topological Neural Operators (TNOs), a principled framework for operator learning on cell complexes that lifts neural operators (NOs) from functions on points and/or edges to topological domains. TNOs represent data as features defined on cells of varying dimension and model their interactions through Discrete Exterior Calculus, enabling explicit cross-dimensional coupling via gradient-, curl-, and divergence-type operators. The key design principle is to decouple where information
multiSourceItems.summary: We introduce Topological Neural Operators (TNOs), a principled framework for operator learning on cell complexes that lifts neural operators (NOs) from functions on points and/or edges to topological domains. TNOs represent data as features defined on cells of varying dimension and model their interactions through Discrete Exterior Calculus, enabling explicit cross-dimensional coupling via gradient-, curl-, and divergence-type operators. The key design principle is to decouple where information flows, as governed by fixed topological operators, from how it is transformed (which is learned), yielding models that respect the geometric support of physical quantities and expose conservation and compatibility structure. We further propose Hierarchical TNOs (HTNOs), which incorporate learned coarse complexes to propagate long-range and topology-dependent information. Our framework subsumes existing NOs as a special case, providing a unified perspective on operator learning across discretizations. Across a range of PDE benchmarks, including irregular-geometry flow problems, TNOs and HTNOs improve accuracy; controlled studies further isolate the benefits of native higher-rank and topological structure. Project page: https://circle-group.github.io/research/TNO
multiSourceItems.author: Lennart Bastian, Samuel Leventhal, Mustafa Hajij, Tolga Birdal

multiSourceItems.title: Echo-Memory: A Controlled Study of Memory in Action World Models
multiSourceItems.description: We present \textbf{Echo-Memory}, a controlled study of memory mechanisms in action-conditioned world models. These models generate multi-segment videos from a first frame, text prompt, and camera-action sequence, but their central failure is often memory rather than local image synthesis: after the camera leaves and returns, the scene or salient object may silently change. Existing memory designs are hard to compare because gains are entangled with backbone, training, retrieval, and evaluation d
multiSourceItems.summary: We present \textbf{Echo-Memory}, a controlled study of memory mechanisms in action-conditioned world models. These models generate multi-segment videos from a first frame, text prompt, and camera-action sequence, but their central failure is often memory rather than local image synthesis: after the camera leaves and returns, the scene or salient object may silently change. Existing memory designs are hard to compare because gains are entangled with backbone, training, retrieval, and evaluation differences. Echo-Memory fixes the action-to-video interface and varies only how history is stored and read by the generator. Under a shared video diffusion backbone, optimizer, camera-action representation, sampler, and evaluation pipeline, we compare raw context, compression-based memory, spatial summaries with different read-out paths, and state-space recurrence. This matched matrix separates four otherwise conflated axes: \emph{capacity}, \emph{compression}, \emph{read-out}, and \emph{recurrence}. We also evaluate memory through a three-branch protocol: replay quality, in-domain loop revisit, and open-domain return probes. The branches routinely disagree, showing that replay fidelity is not a sufficient proxy for remembering a world. Three findings follow. Raw context is a strong capacity baseline and improves open-domain return far more than it improves replay metrics. Compactness is not a free substitute for capacity: aggressive spatial and hybrid-compression memories lose the salient evidence needed for return. Finally, block-wise state-space recurrence is the strongest open-domain return mechanism in our matrix, showing that the structure of implicit memory matters as much as the decision to use it. These results provide a compact protocol for studying memory in action world models beyond isolated replay metrics.
multiSourceItems.author: Wayne King, Zeyue Xue, Yuxuan Bian, Jie Huang, Haoran Li, Yaowei Li, Yaofeng Su, Yuming Li, Haoyu Wang, Shiyi Zhang, Songchun Zhang, Yuwei Niu, Sihan Xu, Junhao Zhuang, Haoyang Huang, Nan Duan

multiSourceItems.title: FASE: Fast Adaptive Semantic Entropy for Code Quality
multiSourceItems.description: Multi-agent code generation offers a promising paradigm for autonomous software development by simulating the human software engineering lifecycle. However, system reliability remains hindered by LLM hallucinations and error propagation across interacting agents. While semantic entropy provides a principled way to quantify uncertainty without ground-truth answers, current methods often rely on costly LLM-driven equivalence checks. In this work, we introduce Fast Adaptive Semantic Entropy (FASE),
multiSourceItems.summary: Multi-agent code generation offers a promising paradigm for autonomous software development by simulating the human software engineering lifecycle. However, system reliability remains hindered by LLM hallucinations and error propagation across interacting agents. While semantic entropy provides a principled way to quantify uncertainty without ground-truth answers, current methods often rely on costly LLM-driven equivalence checks. In this work, we introduce Fast Adaptive Semantic Entropy (FASE), a novel metric that approximates functional correctness based on the minimum spanning tree of structural and semantic dissimilarity graphs. Evaluations on HumanEval and BigCodeBench demonstrate that FASE outperforms state-of-the-art semantic entropy by LLM entailment, achieving a 25% average improvement in Spearman correlation and a 19% increase in ROCAUC score against Pass@1 from ground-truth test cases when using the Qwen3-Embedding-8B model. Furthermore, by eliminating costly LLM-driven equivalence evaluation, FASE incurs negligible computational overhead, requiring only approximately 0.3% of the runtime cost of traditional semantic entropy approaches. These results position FASE as a practical, cost-effective solution for optimizing uncertainty quantification in real-world multi-agent workflows.
multiSourceItems.author: Shizhe Lin, Ladan Tahvildari
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, agent, multi-agent, rag, code generation

multiSourceItems.title: Who Earns the Safety? Intervention-Aware Quantum Predictive Control with Safety Attribution
multiSourceItems.description: Hard safety filters are increasingly placed downstream of learned controllers to guarantee constraint satisfaction at run time. Yet a filtered controller that never violates a constraint may still have learned nothing about safety: the filter can silently repair an incompetent upstream policy, so that post-filter success measures the filter, not the policy. We argue that safe policy learning should ask who earns the safety - the policy or its protective layers - and we make this question measura
multiSourceItems.summary: Hard safety filters are increasingly placed downstream of learned controllers to guarantee constraint satisfaction at run time. Yet a filtered controller that never violates a constraint may still have learned nothing about safety: the filter can silently repair an incompetent upstream policy, so that post-filter success measures the filter, not the policy. We argue that safe policy learning should ask who earns the safety - the policy or its protective layers - and we make this question measurable. We introduce Intervention-Aware Variational Quantum Differentiable Predictive Control (IA-VQC-DPC), which (i) trains a compact variational quantum circuit (VQC) policy under a primal-dual intervention budget that penalizes reliance on a differentiable Control-Barrier-Function (CBF) projection, and (ii) is evaluated with a safety-attribution protocol that decomposes the executed-trajectory correction into a CBF term and a deployment runtime-guard term, and stress-tests the policy with guard-off evaluation. On closed-loop, high-fidelity BOPTEST building-control emulators (5 seeds, 60 episodes per method), intervention-aware training significantly lowers the quantum policy's raw pre-filter violation and total safety-layer reliance (both p < 10^-4) with no significant energy regression; at an equal approximately 400-parameter budget the quantum policy is significantly safer and more comfortable than a matched classical policy. Guard-off evaluation confirms the improvement is policy-level and exposes a valuable negative result: a learned differentiable energy head is only safe when paired with a distribution-aware runtime guard. The attribution protocol is general beyond quantum policies and buildings.
multiSourceItems.author: Yifan Wang
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: safety, evaluation

multiSourceItems.title: SIGA: Self-Evolving Coding-Agent Adapters for Scientific Simulation
multiSourceItems.description: Advanced scientific simulators expose specialized input languages that turn simulation goals into executable configurations, but learning them can cost domain scientists hours to days. We study simulator setup as a problem of agent-tool interface grounding: what minimal simulator-specific adaptations are needed for an off-the-shelf coding agent to operate real scientific software? Our intuition is that coding agents already know how to navigate files, edit code, run commands, and repair outputs,
multiSourceItems.summary: Advanced scientific simulators expose specialized input languages that turn simulation goals into executable configurations, but learning them can cost domain scientists hours to days. We study simulator setup as a problem of agent-tool interface grounding: what minimal simulator-specific adaptations are needed for an off-the-shelf coding agent to operate real scientific software? Our intuition is that coding agents already know how to navigate files, edit code, run commands, and repair outputs, but they lack the simulator's executable contract: its vocabulary, structural constraints, validation rules, and termination conditions. We introduce SIGA, a Simulator-Interface Grounding Adapter that supplies this contract through retrieval, procedural memory, in-trajectory validation, and validation-enforced termination. We primarily evaluate SIGA on GEOS, an open-source multiphysics simulator used in subsurface science. SIGA produces a complete GEOS deck in about five minutes with TreeSim above 0.90, matching an extended-budget human expert who took about three hours, a roughly 36x wall-clock speedup. On a harder held-out set, grounding raises TreeSim from 0.720 to 0.789, a roughly 10% relative gain over the bare agent, and can reduce the across-seed standard deviation by 16x. Self-evolution further improves SIGA by rewriting adapter contents from prior trajectories, yielding the highest held-out GEOS mean and matching or outperforming the strongest hand-designed configuration. Transfers to OpenFOAM and LAMMPS show that the dominant mechanism shifts by interface: validation matters most when structural completeness is the bottleneck, while memory and retrieval matter most when domain correctness is the bottleneck. These results suggest that lightweight, self-improvable grounding layers can turn general coding agents into practical operators of scientific software.
multiSourceItems.author: Matthew Ho, Brian Liu, Jixuan Chen, Audrey Wang, Lianhui Qin
multiSourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent, coding agent

multiSourceItems.title: Discovering Functionally Selective Brain Regions with a Deep Topographic Multimodal Model
multiSourceItems.description: Nearby neurons in cortex share similar response profiles, producing systematic spatial organization across sensory and cognitive systems. Recent topographic models reproduce aspects of this structure but remain unimodal and spatially constrain each layer separately, yielding fragmented maps that capture neither the contiguity of cortical processing streams nor their integration across modalities. We introduce Topo-Omni, a topographic multimodal model in which visual, auditory, and language/cogni
multiSourceItems.summary: Nearby neurons in cortex share similar response profiles, producing systematic spatial organization across sensory and cognitive systems. Recent topographic models reproduce aspects of this structure but remain unimodal and spatially constrain each layer separately, yielding fragmented maps that capture neither the contiguity of cortical processing streams nor their integration across modalities. We introduce Topo-Omni, a topographic multimodal model in which visual, auditory, and language/cognitive processing share a single contiguous in-silico sheet. Built by fine-tuning a pretrained foundation model with a spatial smoothness objective, this architecture develops clusters across modalities that are consistent with human neuroimaging, from sensory to cognitive systems. Driving or suppressing a cluster selectively biases or impairs perception, paralleling human intervention studies. Finally, we use our model to screen for novel clusters in-silico and discover new natural landscape and animal networks which we validate in human data. A single spatial principle thus organizes representations across modalities and processing stages, yielding testable hypotheses about cortical organization.
multiSourceItems.author: Badr AlKhamissi, Johannes Mehrer, Lara Marinov, Ahmed Abdelaal, Abdulkadir Gokce, Martin Schrimpf

multiSourceItems.title: Data Synthesis and Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning for Low-Resource NMT: A Case Study on Q'eqchi' Mayan
multiSourceItems.description: Neural machine translation for digitally low-resource Indigenous languages is often hindered by extreme data scarcity, prompting reliance on extractive web-scraping. To ensure data sovereignty, this study introduces a data synthesis methodology to bootstrap NMT models without scraping target-language parallel text. Focusing on Q'eqchi' Mayan, we transformed community-sourced dictionaries into a massive synthetic corpus, utilizing Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) via LoRA adapters on an mT5
multiSourceItems.summary: Neural machine translation for digitally low-resource Indigenous languages is often hindered by extreme data scarcity, prompting reliance on extractive web-scraping. To ensure data sovereignty, this study introduces a data synthesis methodology to bootstrap NMT models without scraping target-language parallel text. Focusing on Q'eqchi' Mayan, we transformed community-sourced dictionaries into a massive synthetic corpus, utilizing Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) via LoRA adapters on an mT5-base model. In-domain evaluation demonstrates high structural acquisition (BLEU 42.02), proving that synthetic constraints effectively teach complex agglutinative morphology and VOS word order. However, evaluation against an organic glossary reveals a structural-semantic gap (BLEU 0.59), where the model maintains grammatical integrity but lacks the lexical grounding of natural language. The model exhibits overfitting to the constrained structural variance of the synthetic templates; despite high semantic entropy in the pipeline, it struggles with the syntactic fluidity of natural language, forcing organic inputs into rigid learned patterns. Furthermore, an ablation study utilizing a Multi-Task Learning architecture resulted in negative transfer, suggesting that auxiliary tasks competed for limited parameter capacity within the LoRA adapters, causing over-optimization for synthetic markers at the expense of organic flexibility. Ultimately, we establish that synthetic bootstrapping is a highly effective structural primer, but requires authentic data for semantic refinement via Curriculum Learning.
multiSourceItems.author: Alexander Chulzhanov, Soeren Eberhardt, Arjun Mukherjee

multiSourceItems.title: iOSWorld: A Benchmark for Personally Intelligent Phone Agents
multiSourceItems.description: A useful phone agent needs to be personally intelligent. It should reason over a user's identity, history, and preferences as they exist on the device, not just follow isolated instructions in an impersonal sandbox. Existing mobile agent benchmarks lack this kind of personalization. We introduce iOSWorld, the first interactive native iOS simulator benchmark built around a persistent user identity spanning 26 newly built iOS apps. These apps contain connected data such as transactions, messages,
multiSourceItems.summary: A useful phone agent needs to be personally intelligent. It should reason over a user's identity, history, and preferences as they exist on the device, not just follow isolated instructions in an impersonal sandbox. Existing mobile agent benchmarks lack this kind of personalization. We introduce iOSWorld, the first interactive native iOS simulator benchmark built around a persistent user identity spanning 26 newly built iOS apps. These apps contain connected data such as transactions, messages, travel records, social relationships, and financial activity. iOSWorld includes 133 tasks across three increasingly difficult categories. Single-app tasks (27) test one app, multi-app tasks (60) span 2 to 8 apps, and memory and personalization tasks (46) require agents to infer patterns from personal data. We evaluate frontier and open-source computer-use models in both vision-only and privileged vision+XML settings. The best configuration reaches 52\% overall but only 37\% on multi-app tasks. Privileged vision+XML access improves frontier models by up to 26 percentage points, while smaller models do not benefit from added accessibility-tree input. We release iOSWorld as an open-source benchmark with all apps, seeded data, tasks, rubrics, and evaluation code.
multiSourceItems.author: Lawrence Keunho Jang, Mareks Woodside, Geronimo Carom, Andrew Keunwoo Jang, Jing Yu Koh, Ruslan Salakhutdinov

multiSourceItems.title: Preserving Plasticity in Continual Learning via Dynamical Isometry
multiSourceItems.description: Continual training of deep neural networks under non-stationarity often leads to a progressive loss of plasticity, eventually limiting further learning. We relate plasticity to the empirical Neural Tangent Kernel, and identify dynamical isometry (the condition that layer-wise Jacobian singular values remain close to one) as a key mechanism for preserving plasticity in continual learning. We revisit a class of networks that are almost-everywhere isometric while remaining universal Lipschitz funct
multiSourceItems.summary: Continual training of deep neural networks under non-stationarity often leads to a progressive loss of plasticity, eventually limiting further learning. We relate plasticity to the empirical Neural Tangent Kernel, and identify dynamical isometry (the condition that layer-wise Jacobian singular values remain close to one) as a key mechanism for preserving plasticity in continual learning. We revisit a class of networks that are almost-everywhere isometric while remaining universal Lipschitz function approximators, demonstrating that near-dynamical isometry is compatible with expressive nonlinear representations. For general architectures, we propose an efficient isometry-promoting regularization scheme and identify a novel mechanism by which it can reactivate dormant ReLU units. Building on this, we introduce AdamO, an Adam-style adaptive optimizer that decouples isometry regularization from gradient updates, analogous to AdamW. We further reinterpret prior plasticity-preserving approaches through the lens of dynamical isometry, showing that they target only a partial measure of isometry. Across supervised and reinforcement-learning continual-learning benchmarks designed to induce plasticity loss, our methods consistently match or outperform existing approaches.
multiSourceItems.author: Andries Rosseau, Robert Müller, Ann Nowé

items.summary: 腾讯混元推出UniRL，一个支持统一多模态模型的强化学习基础设施，并发布两个新算法DRPO和Flow-DPPO。UniRL通过单个后训练循环（生成→评分→优势→更新→同步）覆盖扩散/流匹配模型、LLM/VLM及统一多模态模型（如Hunyuan-Image 3和Bagel）。模型与算法作为独立轴，可实现模型×算法的组合覆盖。框架支持可插拔rollout引擎（训练侧/SGLang/vLLM-Omni）、FSDP2分片和三种部署模式。FlowDPPO针对流/扩散模型引入基于精确散度的信任域策略优化；DRPO为LLM RL提供平滑的优势加权二次正则化方法。代码已开源。

trendEntities.title: 火山引擎TRAE Work企业版正式上线，面向全员提供AI办公平台
normalizedKeys: title:火山引擎trae work企业版正式上线 面向全员提供ai办公平台

items.title: 火山引擎TRAE Work企业版正式上线，面向全员提供AI办公平台
items.summary: 火山引擎将TRAE Solo品牌升级为TRAE Work企业版，发布面向企业的AI办公平台。平台提供Work和Code两种模式：Work模式面向产品、运营、市场等非技术岗位，支持上传.pptx、.xlsx、图片等多种格式混合输入直接输出PPT或文档，支持语音讨论自动整理结构化纪要，支持按天或按周自动运行的数据整理和报告生成；Code模式面向开发者和业务同学，可用自然语言描述需求直接生成页面或小应用。桌面端、网页端和移动端多端同步。企业后台可统一配置模型、用量限额、上传内部文档，沙箱机制隔离执行，支持命令黑名单、MCP白名单、内容安全策略，关键操作可审计。同时发起TRAE Enterprise Fellow招募计划。

trendEntities.title: Kimi 预测全部104场世界杯比赛：德国或被低估
normalizedKeys: title:kimi 预测全部104场世界杯比赛 德国或被低估

items.title: Kimi 预测全部104场世界杯比赛：德国或被低估
items.summary: Kimi 利用 Agent Swarm 系统并行协调300个子智能体，分析战术、球员状态、伤病、赛程、天气、赔率等因素，预测2026年美加墨世界杯全部104场比赛，并发布每轮赛前预测和赛后回顾。模型层融合了 Elo/FIFA 强度、Poisson 进球分布、xG/xT 指标、蒙特卡洛模拟等方法。预测结果显示西班牙和法国为头号热门，但德国夺冠概率可能被市场低估：模型基线估计约11.0%，校准估计约11.3%，而部分市场隐含概率仅约7.4%，正向偏差约+3.6个百分点。该判断基于多分析链交叉验证，可能源于对德国近两届小组出局的近因偏差以及纳格尔斯曼高位压迫体系与穆西亚拉/维尔茨新创造轴的复苏信号。

items.title: 可灵AI×候鸟300发起AI主义新浪潮AIGC影像大赛
items.summary: 可灵AI与候鸟300联合发起AIGC影像大赛。线下大赛6月16-26日在阿那亚海边举办，评审包括李少红、陈明昊、焦雄屏等影视与AI专家及美院教授，设10万奖金+超200万灵感值，结果6月27日晚揭晓。线上大赛6月22日前投稿可角逐"最受关注大奖"，获奖者受邀参加6月27日颁奖。参赛要求：视频≥50%由可灵AI生成，官网投稿填表并带话题分享。

trendEntities.title: 一个Agent如何通过链式调用两个HuggingFace Space构建3D巴黎画廊
normalizedKeys: title:一个agent如何通过链式调用两个huggingface space构建3d巴黎画廊

items.title: 一个Agent如何通过链式调用两个HuggingFace Space构建3D巴黎画廊
items.summary: 一个编码Agent调用HuggingFace上的两个Space，从零构建了展示巴黎地标3D高斯散点图的交互式画廊。Agent先用ideogram-ai/ideogram4生成每个纪念碑的黑色背景图像，再通过VAST-AI/TripoSplat从单张图像重建3D高斯散点（.ply），自动完成坐标系校正、取景、压缩为.ksplat（体积缩小约3倍），并构建基于Three.js的滚动切换、拖拽旋转查看器，最终部署为静态Space。整个过程无需客户端库，每个Space通过`agents.md`暴露可调用API。

items.title: 台湾考虑限制AI芯片对华出口以配合美国
items.summary: 据知情人士透露，台湾当局正考虑对AI芯片出口中国大陆实施更严格的管制，以进一步与美国出口限制措施对齐。此举旨在遏制半导体走私，但可能招致北京方面的谴责。

sourceItems.summary: 据知情人士透露，台湾当局正考虑对AI芯片出口中国大陆实施更严格的管制，以进一步与美国出口限制措施对齐。此举旨在遏制半导体走私，但可能招致北京方面的谴责。

items.title: 仅凭一份文档，Qwen3.7-Max 从 0 交付双端应用
items.summary: 在无设计稿和后端代码的条件下，Qwen3.7-Max 仅凭一份约 15 万字的产品调研文档，于隔离环境中全自动完成移动端与 Web 端两套真实应用从 0 到 1 交付，单端耗时约 4 小时，中途无人工接管。模型不具备图像理解能力，通过像素坐标反推布局约束实现界面还原。实验采用"分阶段注入约束→逐层验收→带错纠正"的闭环控制系统：任务拆分为规划、架构、编码等阶段，验收覆盖静态检查、编译自检（0 error）、路由完整性（Web 端 34 条路由全部可达）、功能扫描及真机冷启动冒烟。失败时错误文本自动注入下一轮重试，使模型数小时内收敛。移动端产出可安装 APK，Web 端 typecheck 与构建均通过。

trendEntities.title: NeuroBait：微调AI助手，为ADHD大脑点燃多巴胺火花
normalizedKeys: title:neurobait 微调ai助手 为adhd大脑点燃多巴胺火花

items.title: NeuroBait：微调AI助手，为ADHD大脑点燃多巴胺火花
items.summary: NeuroBait是基于Google gemma-3-12b-it微调的AI对话助手，旨在帮助ADHD患者克服"知道该做什么但无法开始"的执行功能障碍。采用16-bit LoRA（r=16， alpha=16）在Unsloth上训练3个epoch，学习率2e-4，最大序列长度2048，使用单张H100 80GB GPU。数据集为基于真实ADHD场景手工合成的少量数据。部署于Hugging Face Space（ZeroGPU），使用Gradio和标准transformers+peft，运行时以4-bit NF4加载基础模型并应用LoRA适配器。NeuroBait不生成待办清单，而是根据用户上下文给出3-6句温暖流畅的回复，引导用户找到一件微小可立即执行的动作，从而激发多巴胺、降低启动阻力。

trendEntities.title: NVIDIA cuTile Python 教程：在 Colab 中构建用于向量加法、矩阵加法和矩阵乘法的 Tiled GPU 内核
normalizedKeys: title:nvidia cutile python 教程 在 colab 中构建用于向量加法 矩阵加法和矩阵乘法的 tiled gpu 内核

items.title: NVIDIA cuTile Python 教程：在 Colab 中构建用于向量加法、矩阵加法和矩阵乘法的 Tiled GPU 内核
items.summary: 该教程基于 NVIDIA cuTile Python 实现了分块 GPU 内核编程工作流，在 Colab 环境中配置 GPU、驱动、CUDA 及 cuTile 可用性后，分别构建了 tiled 向量加法、矩阵加法和矩阵乘法核函数，并以 PyTorch 作为回退保持 notebook 可执行。每一步均通过 PyTorch 验证结果正确性，并基准测试了各阶段的中位运行时间。
items.description: 该教程基于 NVIDIA cuTile Python 实现了分块 GPU 内核编程工作流，在 Colab 环境中配置 GPU、驱动、CUDA 及 cuTile 可用性后，分别构建了 tiled 向量加法、矩阵加法和矩阵乘法核函数，并以 PyTorch 作为回退保持 notebook 可执行。每一步均通过 PyTorch 验证结果正确性，并基准测试了各阶段的中位运行时间。

trendEntities.title: 硅基流动联合CodeWhale推出DeepSeek V4终端最佳性价比组合
normalizedKeys: title:硅基流动联合codewhale推出deepseek v4终端最佳性价比组合

items.title: 硅基流动联合CodeWhale推出DeepSeek V4终端最佳性价比组合
items.summary: 硅基流动宣布，通过V4-Pro（质量）与V4-Flash（速度）两行配置，即可在终端获得DeepSeek V4的最佳性价比组合。专为DeepSeek V4构建的终端编码智能体CodeWhale现已内置SiliconFlow。CodeWhale具备流式推理（显示思考过程）、自动路由（根据任务复杂度切换模型与思考深度）、零漂移（通过书面宪法为每轮排序权威，保持V4定向）以及自我改进（V4协助编写框架，框架提升后每个会话更强大）等特性。

trendEntities.title: 中国准备2950亿美元计划资助全国AI基础设施建设
normalizedKeys: title:中国准备2950亿美元计划资助全国ai基础设施建设

items.title: 中国准备2950亿美元计划资助全国AI基础设施建设
items.summary: 中国计划在未来五年投入约2万亿元人民币（约2950亿美元）建设全国数据中心，以推动国内AI产业发展并超越美国。该投资将覆盖数据中心基础设施的大规模建设，为北京在关键技术领域的雄心提供资金支持。

trendEntities.title: AI 编程独角兽 Cursor 欧洲总部落子伦敦，SpaceX 手握 600 亿美元收购选择权
normalizedKeys: title:ai 编程独角兽 cursor 欧洲总部落子伦敦 spacex 手握 600 亿美元收购选择权

items.title: AI 编程独角兽 Cursor 欧洲总部落子伦敦，SpaceX 手握 600 亿美元收购选择权
items.summary: Cursor 将欧洲总部设在伦敦，计划招聘约 200 名员工，并在巴黎、慕尼黑等地开设小型办事处。SpaceX 拥有以 600 亿美元收购 Cursor 的选择权，或支付 100 亿美元开展全新合作。Cursor 目前 B2B 年化营收约 26 亿美元，客户包括英国航空、英国石油、诺基亚等。公司强调数据留存欧洲本地以满足监管合规，其平台支持用户用自然语言生成代码，主打模型中立，竞争对手包括 GitHub Copilot、OpenAI 和谷歌的同类产品。

trendEntities.title: 小米 MiMo 与 TileRT 联合发布 UltraSpeed 模式，1T 模型输出突破 1000 tokens/s
normalizedKeys: title:小米 mimo 与 tilert 联合发布 ultraspeed 模式 1t 模型输出突破 1000 tokens s

items.title: 小米 MiMo 与 TileRT 联合发布 UltraSpeed 模式，1T 模型输出突破 1000 tokens/s
items.summary: 小米 MiMo 与 TileRT 联合发布 MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed 模式，使 1T 参数旗舰模型输出速度首次突破 1000 tokens/s。模型侧采用 FP4 混合量化（仅量化 MoE Expert）与 DFlash 块级 masked 并行推测解码（coding 场景平均接受长度 6.30 tokens）；系统侧 TileRT 引入常驻内核引擎与异构流水线协作。API 限时开放（2026 年 6 月 9 日至 23 日），定价为 MiMo-V2.5-Pro 的 3 倍，速度提升约 10 倍。FP4 权重与 DFlash 模型 checkpoint 已开源至 HuggingFace。
items.description: 小米 MiMo 与 TileRT 联合发布 MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed 模式，使 1T 参数旗舰模型输出速度首次突破 1000 tokens/s。模型侧采用 FP4 混合量化（仅量化 MoE Expert）与 DFlash 块级 masked 并行推测解码（coding 场景平均接受长度 6.30 tokens）；系统侧 TileRT 引入常驻内核引擎与异构流水线协作。API 限时开放（2026 年 6 月 9 日至 23 日），定价为 MiMo-V2.5-Pro 的 3 倍，速度提升约 10 倍。FP4 权重与 DFlash 模型 checkpoint 已开源至 HuggingFace。

trendEntities.whyItMatters: 小米 MiMo 与 TileRT 联合发布 MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed 模式，使 1T 参数旗舰模型输出速度首次突破 1000 tokens/s。模型侧采用 FP4 混合量化（仅量化 MoE Expert）与 DFlash 块级 masked 并行推测解码（coding 场景平均接受长度 6.30 tokens）；系统侧 TileRT 引入常驻内核引擎与异构流水线协作。API 限时开放（2026 年 6 月 9 日至 23 日），定价为 MiMo-V2.5-Pro 的 3 倍，速度提升约 10 倍。FP4 权重与 DFlash 模型 checkpoint 已开源至 HuggingFace。

trendEntities.title: OpenAI 秘密提交 IPO 申请，奥特曼旗下 Tools for Humanity 裁员
normalizedKeys: title:openai 秘密提交 ipo 申请 奥特曼旗下 tools for humanity 裁员

items.title: OpenAI 秘密提交 IPO 申请，奥特曼旗下 Tools for Humanity 裁员
items.summary: OpenAI 近日秘密提交 IPO 申请。其 CEO 山姆·奥特曼旗下的 Tools for Humanity 公司正裁员，该公司因虹膜扫描项目 World 及加密货币 Worldcoin 知名，投后估值 25 亿美元，获 Andreessen Horowitz 等投资。因营收困境，公司缩减规模。海外监管方面，肯尼亚以隐私和金融风险为由叫停运营，韩国因违反隐私法规罚款 83 万美元。

trendEntities.title: Elon Musk 详解 SpaceX AI1 轨道 AI 数据中心卫星方案
normalizedKeys: title:elon musk 详解 spacex ai1 轨道 ai 数据中心卫星方案

items.title: Elon Musk 详解 SpaceX AI1 轨道 AI 数据中心卫星方案
items.summary: Elon Musk 首次详细解释 SpaceX 的 AI1 轨道 AI 数据中心卫星：峰值功率 150 kW，持续计算功率约 120 kW，相当于一个 NVIDIA GB300 机架；太阳能板效率 250 W/m2；双面散热器排热 1，400 W/m2。通过激光链路实现约 1 Tbps 互联，低轨 600-800 km 高度往返延迟 6-8 ms。由 Starship 发射，计划部署多达百万颗卫星，2027 年底前实现量产。近地目标为吉瓦级轨道 AI 算力，长期向太瓦级推进。
